response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist full-stack engineer, but the role's core mandatory requirements are essentially unmet: professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 versus the required 5-10 years, there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle work (only MySQL/MongoDB), and there is no Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain background. The brief fintech stint at RIDE Capital involved TypeScript/GraphQL, not financial-products engineering. Low-latency DLang work shows systems aptitude, but it does not substitute for the specific C++/Windows and financial-domain depth this consulting engagement demands.
strengths: Solid systems-programming aptitude from low-latency DLang work handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, DevOps tooling, and open-source contributions
Broad, adaptable polyglot experience (TypeScript, Java, DLang, C#) with proven fast ramp-up across domains
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, capital markets, or investment banking domain experience
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
